Add iTipFooty.com.au auto-tipping integration

Automates submitting prediction engine picks to iTipFooty:

- Login via POST to services/login.php with session cookie management
- Parse tipping page HTML to extract game numbers, team names, form fields
- Map prediction engine picks (team IDs) to iTipFooty H/A selections
- Submit tips via POST to services/SubmitTips.php
- Handle locked games (already kicked off) gracefully

Backend:
- New service: backend/src/services/itipfooty.ts
- New route: /api/itipfooty/status and /api/itipfooty/submit
- Credentials loaded from .env (ITIPFOOTY_USERNAME, PASSWORD, COMP_ID)
- dotenv added for env var loading
- Submission logged to DataSourceLog

Frontend:
- Settings page: iTipFooty Integration card with status display
- Submit Tips button with detailed result feedback per game
- Shows configuration instructions when not configured

Security:
- Credentials stored in backend/.env (gitignored)
- .env.example provided with empty placeholders
- Status endpoint masks username with ***

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Centralize card motion presets and UI tokens, add reduced-motion support (#25)

* Initial plan

* refactor: centralize card motion presets and UI tokens, improve GPU friendliness

- Create lib/ui/tokens.ts with shared card sizing (2:3 aspect ratio),
  perspective, hover lift, reveal scale, and eliminated opacity constants
- Create lib/motion/presets.ts with spring (hover/fan/stack), flip
  (normal/instant), timing (fast/normal/dramatic), and reduced-motion
  (INSTANT) motion presets
- Refactor InteractiveCard, CardFanHand, CardStack to consume shared
  presets and tokens instead of inline duplicated configs
- Add useReducedMotion() from framer-motion for accessibility
- Add will-change: transform on actively animating layers
- Align card sizes to exact 2:3 ratio (56×84, 80×120, 112×168)
- Slightly tighten stack reveal from 0.6s to 0.5s via TIMING_DRAMATIC
- No public API, prop, or card engine logic changes

fix: Use AMPLIHACK_AGENT_BINARY env var instead of hardcoded claude (#3100)

* fix: use AMPLIHACK_AGENT_BINARY env var instead of hardcoded claude

The recipe runner and delegation scripts hardcoded 'claude' as the agent
binary, causing failures when amplihack is run with other agents (copilot,
codex, etc.).

Changes:
- cli.py: Set AMPLIHACK_AGENT_BINARY env var at dispatch for all 5 agent cmds
- rust_runner.py: Forward --agent-binary flag to Rust recipe runner
- platform_cli.py: Read env var instead of hardcoding 'claude'
- delegate_response.py (3 mirrors): Read env var + add missing 'import os'

The Rust recipe runner companion changes are in
rysweet/amplihack-recipe-runner#29.

Fixes #3099

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address review findings - empty string edge case and missed hardcoded files

- Fix orchestrator.py: use ${AMPLIHACK_AGENT_BINARY:-claude} bash fallback
- Fix executor.py (2 locations): same bash fallback pattern for remote execution
- Fix empty string edge case: use 'or "claude"' pattern instead of default param
  in platform_cli.py and all 3 delegate_response.py mirrors

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Phase 4 complete: full thesis first draft (5 chapters, ~12,000 words)

thesis/chapter1-introduction.md (~3,000 words):
- Motivation, research questions, chaos engineering background
- Kubernetes security model (PSA, NetworkPolicy, Falco)
- Literature review: Chaos Mesh, kube-bench, Gremlin, Trivy, gap analysis
- Ethical considerations

thesis/chapter2-methodology.md (~2,000 words):
- Design science research approach
- 5 Whys RCA motivating requirements
- MoSCoW requirements (Must/Should/Could/Won't)
- Technology selection rationale (Go, Kind, Calico, Falco, Helm)
- Experimental design (golden vs misconfigured cluster)
- Validity threats (internal, external, construct)

thesis/chapter3-architecture.md (~3,500 words):
- Full system architecture diagram
- All 5 internal packages documented
- PSA admission edge case implementation
- AlertStore thread-safety and ClearRule design
- MTTD calculation rationale
- Falco Helm chart compatibility issues and fixes
- 27-test testing strategy

thesis/chapter4-results.md (~2,500 words):
- RQ1: 100% detection, two control layers validated
- RQ2: ~4,200x faster than manual, reproducible
- RQ3: MTTD architecture sound; null in local runs (deployment constraint)
- Threats to validity: network-egress Kind limitation, sample size

thesis/chapter5-conclusion.md (~1,000 words):
- Contributions summary
- Limitations
- Future work: expanded experiments, cloud SIEM, parallel execution
- Reflection on chaos engineering applied to security

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: rewrite deploy workflow using official fastapi cloud pattern

The CLI reads FASTAPI_CLOUD_TOKEN and FASTAPI_CLOUD_APP_ID from env
vars directly (via Identity._get_token_from_env). No auth.json writing,
no login step, no cloud.json needed.

Uses astral-sh/setup-uv@v7 and actions/checkout@v5 (also fixes the
Node.js 20 deprecation warnings).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: fix auth by writing auth.json instead of broken --token flag

fastapi cloud login has no --token option. Instead write the PAT
directly into ~/.local/share/fastapi-cli/auth.json (Linux XDG path),
matching how the CLI stores credentials locally.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: LingoSummarApi

commit.message:
fix: allow optional percentage in summarize schemas (#2)

* fix: allow optional percentage in summarize schemas

SummarizeRequest and SummarizeAgainRequest had `percentage: int` with
`ge=30, le=80`, which rejected `0` or `null` from the frontend with a
422 Unprocessable Entity.

Changed both to `int | None` with `ge=1, le=100` so callers can omit
or null-out the field; `_get_percentage()` already handles the
default fallback to a random 30-80 value.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: widen percentage range to 1-100 across models and simplify fallback

- Text model: percentage ge=30,le=80 → ge=1,le=100
- Summary model: same range update
- _get_percentage: replace random 30-80 fallback with deterministic
  default of 50%; respect user's explicit choice via schema validation
- Remove unused `random` import

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: add GitHub Actions workflow to auto-deploy to FastAPI Cloud

Triggers on every push to main. Installs fastapi-cli, restores the
auth token from the FASTAPI_CLOUD_AUTH GitHub secret, then runs
`fastapi deploy . --no-wait` which uses the app_id from .fastapicloud/cloud.json.

Setup required:
1. Copy the contents of ~/.fastapi/auth.json on your local machine
2. Add it as a GitHub secret named FASTAPI_CLOUD_AUTH in the repo settings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: fix deploy workflow to use uv + PAT token auth

- Use uv sync to install deps (matches project toolchain)
- Authenticate with `fastapi cloud login --token` using PAT
- Remove incorrect auth.json approach
- Run fastapi via `uv run` to use project venv

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: recreate .fastapicloud/cloud.json in CI before deploy

Since .fastapicloud is gitignored, the folder won't exist after
checkout. Recreate cloud.json from GitHub secrets so `fastapi deploy .`
can find the app_id and team_id.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Document cluster node health feature (#6502)

Adds documentation for the node health status feature implemented in PRs #4758 and #4767,
which enables Valkey-compatible cluster node state tracking.

Important: Node health is passive metadata provided by the cluster manager (control plane) via DFLYCLUSTER CONFIG.
Dragonfly nodes do not actively determine their own health status.

Refresh predictions after data scrape and historical import

- Add useEffect to watch historical import completion and refresh
  predictions, ladder, fixtures, and logs when import finishes
- Historical import button was already present in Settings UI

any scrape operation, especially after historical import which
populates the 2024-2025 data needed for 6 prediction factors.

commit.message:
Fix TeamStat enrichment and team ID mapping

Backend:
- Fix TEAM_ID_MAP: corrected NRL.com numeric team IDs based on
  actual API responses (Sharks=500028, Dolphins=500723, Roosters=500001).
  Old map had Sharks mapped to Dolphins' slot, causing CRO fixtures
  to be stored as DOL and CRO missing entirely from TeamStat.
- Fix enrichment roundId mismatch: enrichment step now looks up the
  actual roundId used by computeTeamStats() instead of deriving it
  from the ladder (which could be a different round).
- Add penaltyCount to Playing Statistics prediction factor (~0.15
  weight, lower is better — inverted like errors).

Frontend:
- Add Penalties column to stats comparison grid in Predictions.tsx
  (grid-cols-4 → grid-cols-5).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix current round detection and isCurrent flag updates

- scrapeCurrentRound() now auto-detects the current round from the NRL
  API (selectedRoundId) instead of relying on the stale isCurrent DB flag
- Also scrapes the previous round to pick up completed results
- fetchDraw() now reads isCurrentRound from API fixtures and updates
  the Round.isCurrent flag accordingly, clearing it from other rounds
- scrapeFixtures() delegates to scrapeCurrentRound() when no round
  specified instead of using stale DB lookup
- Cleaned up stale R1 fixture data (duplicate/wrong matchups)

Before: R1 had 15 fixtures (7 stale + 8 real), R2 had 0, isCurrent
stuck on R1. After: R1=8 completed, R2=8 (1 live + 7 upcoming),
isCurrent correctly on R2.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: footy-tips-app

commit.message:
Fix fetchTeamStats to parse NRL leaderboard API format

The NRL /stats/data endpoint returns stats organized by category
(Scoring, Possession, Defence, etc.) with top-N leaderboards per stat,
not a flat team-per-row array. Rewrote fetchTeamStats() to:

1. Always compute base stats (W/L/D, PF/PA) from fixture data first
2. Then enrich with API leaderboard data: completion%, possession%,
   errors, penalties, and computed tackle efficiency
3. Tackle efficiency derived from Tackles/(Tackles+Missed Tackles)
4. Gracefully handles partial data (API only returns top teams per stat)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: footy-tips-app

commit.message:
Add TeamStat metrics scraping and prediction factor

Backend:
- Add fetchTeamStats() to nrl-api.ts: fetches from NRL /stats/data
  endpoint with auto-discovery of response format and field mapping
- Add computeTeamStats() fallback: calculates team stats from
  completed fixture data (W/L, PF/PA, home/away splits, streaks)
- Wire into scraper.ts: new scrapeTeamStats(), included in scrapeAll()
  and scrapeHistorical()
- Add 'team-stats' target to scrape route
- Add 'Playing Statistics' prediction factor (~10pt max) in analysis.ts
  comparing completion rate, tackle efficiency, errors, possession
- Extend TeamProfile with stats fields, fetch latest in buildTeamProfile()

Frontend:
- Add 'Team Stats' scrape target button in Settings.tsx
- Add playing statistics comparison grid in Predictions.tsx expanded
  analysis (completion%, tackle efficiency, errors, possession)

The stats API fetcher gracefully falls back to fixture-computed stats
when the NRL stats endpoint is unavailable or returns unexpected format.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add injury impact factor to prediction engine

- Add position criticality mapping (halfback/fullback/hooker=critical,
  lock/centre/prop=important, winger/second-row=standard, bench=depth)
- Add injury burden scoring: severity × position criticality × status
- Add return-from-injury boost for 'probable' status players
- Integrate as ~10pt max 'Injury Impact' factor in predictMatch()
- Update buildTeamProfile() to fetch active injuries from DB
- Update Predictions.tsx to show injury lists in expanded analysis
- Update MatchDetail.tsx to fetch and display real injury data per team

fix: restore original URL on Stripe cancel and add return_to on success (#158)

* fix: restore original URL on Stripe cancel and add return_to on success

- Accept optional returnUrl in CheckoutInput (GraphQL schema + Zod validation)
- Pass returnUrl from frontend (window.location.href) via useStripeCheckout hook
- Use returnUrl as cancel_url in Stripe session (fallback to /payment/cancel)
- Encode returnUrl as return_to query param on success_url so the success
  page can render a 'Return to page' button
- Update PaymentSuccessPage to read return_to param and conditionally render
  the return button
- Clarify FRONTEND_URL in .env.example must be a base URL with no path suffix

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(security): validate returnUrl origin to prevent open redirect

- checkout.schema.ts: add origin refine to returnUrl Zod validator so
  only URLs matching config.frontendUrl origin are accepted
- stripe.ts: add isSameOrigin() defense-in-depth check before using
  returnUrl as cancel_url/success_url; falls back to /payment/* defaults
- payment/success/page.tsx: add sanitizeReturnUrl() to validate return_to
  query param against FRONTEND_URL origin before rendering as link
- resume.md: fix github → GitHub capitalization in project labels

commit.message:
fix(deps): add en-core-web-sm as explicit dependency for cloud deployment

spaCy model was installed via a separate Dockerfile RUN step, which
doesn't apply when FastAPI Cloud runs uv sync from pyproject.toml
directly. Adding the model as a direct URL dependency ensures it is
included in every environment via uv sync.

- Regenerate uv.lock

commit.message:
fix(config): resolve CORS_ORIGINS SettingsError on startup

pydantic-settings v2 calls json.loads() on list[str] fields before
field_validator runs, causing a SettingsError if the env var is not
valid JSON (e.g. plain URL or comma-separated string in a dashboard).

- Change CORS_ORIGINS type from list[str] to str to bypass pydantic-
  settings' complex-type JSON pre-parsing
- Replace field_validator with a @computed_field property cors_origins
  that accepts JSON arrays, comma-separated values, or a single URL
- Update main.py to use settings.cors_origins (the parsed list)
- Update .env.example to document both accepted formats

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor(api): migrate backend from Flask to FastAPI

- Add Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml for containerised deployment
- Add pyproject.toml and uv.lock replacing requirements.txt
- Refactor summarizer core: engine.py, helpers.py, models.py replacing legacy files

- Add comprehensive test suite: test_health, test_summarize, test_texts with conftest

commit.message:
feat(control-plane): implement artifact and review packet retrieval endpoints

Add REST endpoints for retrieving task artifacts, review history,
review cycle packets, parsed packet content, and merge details.

Endpoints:
- GET /tasks/:taskId/artifacts - artifact tree organized by type
- GET /tasks/:taskId/packets/:packetId - parsed JSON packet content
- GET /tasks/:taskId/reviews - review cycle history with decisions
- GET /tasks/:taskId/reviews/:cycleId/packets - specialist packets
- GET /tasks/:taskId/merge - merge queue item + validation runs

New files:
- review/artifacts.controller.ts + artifacts.service.ts
- review/reviews.controller.ts + reviews.service.ts
- merge/merge-details.controller.ts + merge-details.service.ts
- 6 test files with 31 new tests (3,268 total passing)

Closes T084.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: centralize API key resolution in settings.resolve_api_key

Add resolve_api_key() and resolve_endpoint() to config/settings.py
with canonical env-var fallback chains per provider. All consumers
(providers.py, hallucination_verifier.py, web_search.py, refchecker.py,
settings.get_config) now call the shared functions instead of
duplicating os.getenv chains.

Canonical OpenAI order: OPENAI_API_KEY -> REFCHECKER_OPENAI_API_KEY -> OPENAI_CHAT_KEY

repository.description: A tool that validates academic paper references

commit.message:
feat: LLM-assessed web search with full reference metadata

Replace the simple 'find paper' query with a structured prompt that
sends title, authors, year, and venue to the LLM and asks it to search
the web and return a verdict (EXISTS / NOT_FOUND / UNSURE) with
reasoning.

The checker now parses the LLM verdict to drive scoring instead of
relying solely on URL domain heuristics. This fixes the false-match
problem where topically similar but different papers were counted as
evidence of existence.

Live test results:
  REAL 'Attention Is All You Need' → EXISTS, delta -0.10
  FAKE 'Self-Organizing Transformers...' → NOT_FOUND, delta +0.05

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix overbroad blurring affecting Location and other fields

Removed old blurAdjacentValue that used broad [class*='row'] selector,
which matched containers wrapping multiple essentials items. Removed
overly broad AZURE_SELECTORS that could match non-subscription fields.
Subscription fields are now targeted only by specific aria-label and
essentialsLabel/essentialsValue class patterns.

repository.description: The Hattrick's salary assistant

commit.message:
Fix nationality not updating currency display when restoring form from params URL (#7)

* Initial plan

* Add support for individual player URL parameters (goalkeeping, playmaking, etc.)

Co-authored-by: tagliala <556268+tagliala@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove accidentally committed package-lock.json

Co-authored-by: tagliala <556268+tagliala@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix fillForm() to update WageWizard.LeagueDetails after restoring league from params URL

Co-authored-by: tagliala <556268+tagliala@users.noreply.github.com>

* Include nation in CHPP link; sync per-team league

- Add LeagueID to each team's array in getTeamDetails so multi-team
  users carry the correct league for each team
- In updateCHPPPlayerFields, set WageWizard_League select value and
  WageWizard.LeagueDetails from the active team's LeagueID so that
  formSerialize() captures the correct nation in the generated link,
  even though the league select is hidden in CHPP mode

* test

* Populate league select before setting CHPP nation

createCountryDropbox() was only called in non-CHPP mode, leaving
the WageWizard_League select with no <option> elements in CHPP
mode. Setting .value on an empty select is a no-op, so the team's
country ID was never actually stored in the field and was missing
from the serialized link. Call createCountryDropbox() in the
CHPP OK path before setupCHPPPlayerFields so the select has
options when updateCHPPPlayerFields sets its value.

* Remove named URL params recognition

Drop fillFormFromPlayerParams and PLAYER_URL_PARAMS. The named
?goalkeeping=X&age=Y&nationality=Z parameter scheme is no longer
needed; the standard ?params= serialized format is the only
supported way to pre-fill the form from a URL.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: tagliala <556268+tagliala@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Geremia Taglialatela <tagliala.dev@gmail.com>

commit.message:
Fix profile workdir: use local repo for Copilot boot, skip git ops

Copilot needs a repo with init.ps1 to emit 'Environment loaded:' —
using TEMP caused a 30min boot timeout. Now uses the local repo path
without any git fetch/checkout.

commit.message:
feat(randutil): add random value generation utilities (#607)

New package with 11 random utility functions:
- Int, Float64, Bool: basic random values
- Pick[T]: random element from slice
- Shuffle[T]: random permutation
- Sample[T]: random subset without replacement
- String, Hex, Alpha, AlphaNum: random string generators
- WeightedPick[T]: weighted random selection

All generic functions use math/rand/v2 for performance.
Includes doc.go, 25+ unit tests, 3 benchmarks, 3 examples.

Co-authored-by: Jordan <jordan@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
